INTRODUCTION
High Grade Serous Ovarian Cancer (HGSC)
•The most common and lethal subtype of ovarian cancer1
•Diagnosed at advanced stages in >70% of patients
•High relapse rates despite initial response to therapy
•>50% 5 -year survival rate
Tumour Microenvironment (TME)
•TME contributes to chemoresistance through immune evasion, 
angiogenesis, and cell interactions
•Not only do genetic mutations contribute to tumorigenesis2
•Molecular organization and spatial location play a crucial role
Cell Cycle
•Abnormal cell proliferation is a critical aspect of cancer 
progression
•It constitutes a tumour -intrinsic vulnerability that remains 
unknown in HGSC
OBJECTIVE
To develop a multiomic spatial integration approach to better 
recapitulate the complexity of the HGSC TME and its contribution to 
tumour progressionUnveiling the spatial regulation of tumour progression in ovarian cancer
Sara Palomino -Echeverria1,2,3, Tatiana Belova3, Ada Junquera -Mencía1, Iga Niemiec1, Inga -Maria Launonen1, Ping -an Hsieh3, Anna Vähärautio1,4, Marieke L. Kuijjer3,5,6*, Anniina Färkkilä1,5,7*
1: Research Program in Systems Oncology, University of Helsinki, Helsinki, Finland : 2: Institute for Molecular Medicine Finland (FIMM), Helsinki, Finland ; 3: Computational Biology and Systems Medicine, Centre for Molecular Medicine Norway (NCMM), University of Oslo (UiO), Oslo,

To identify cell-cycle -related phenotypic clusters, we computed an NMF matrix 
using data from the selected ROIs with GeoMx . We selected the 200 most 
variable genes associated with GO:0007049 (cell cycle) and combined them 
with genes encoding proteins used in t-cycIF . For each ROI, a gene signature 
was derived . The NMF was performed using the Brunet method, with a rank of 
10.•Cell Pairing Based on Signature Expression . Match cells across scRNA -
seq and t-CycIF based on shared expression signatures for transcriptomic 
and proteomic alignment . 
•Modelling Transcriptomic Hierarchies . Using scRNA -seq data, construct 
gene regulatory networks linking transcription factors (TFs) to target genes . 
•Revealing new cell clusters resulting from analysing dependencies by 
applying CAVACHON between modalities .CTo find links that allow the integration of t-CycIF and 
scRNA -seq data.
scRNAseq
CNEXT STEPS
We applied the CAVACHON probabilistic learning framework to identify distinct scRNA -seq features . While we 
successfully recapitulated the tumor cell populations identified by Launonen et al.3 our method revealed

c learning framework to identify distinct scRNA -seq features . While we 
successfully recapitulated the tumor cell populations identified by Launonen et al.3 our method revealed 
higher granularity in the isolation of these cells. The next step will involve exploring this heterogeneity and 
investigating its potential link to the cell cycle by conditioning the previously calculated gene signatures .
t-CycIF
CONCLUSIONS
-At least three patient archetypes could be outlined from the tumour cell -cycle 
composition based on protein expression clustering.
-NMF of spatial transcriptomics has the potential to recapitulate the clusters 
observed at protein levels.
-CAVACHON analysis provided enhanced granularity in tumour cell population 
identification. Incorporating additional omics layers would further refine these 
insights and improve overall accuracy.
ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS
